impact:
CONFIRMED VALID. UNBLOCKED as of 2026-07-16 (ISS-008 landed) — but see the capture caveat below.

Original: P1's main fix (C15=8.2n, C17=1.8n) brought 8k/10k within ±1.5dB (was -3.4). Residual at
12.5k/16k (-5.9/-19.1dB) is cumulative bilinear warp of the V2 recovery LPF cascade, not a component
value. Ruled out: C16 470p->330p (overshot +4.7dB at 8k), C14 47n->39n (regressed LF hump to 2.04dB),
C32/C29 22p->15p (zero effect, confirms error is pre-tone-stack), C42 10n->12n (worse). Likely needs
prewarp (utils/Prewarp.h exists, unused) or extending the oversampled region per dsp.md "Top-octave
accuracy".

The BL=1.00 captures reproduce this diagnosis closely (12.9kHz -5.2/-7.3/-1.3dB, 16.3kHz
-25.1/-13.9/-18.1dB across the three V2 BL=1.00 captures) — the wet-path top-octave deficit is real
and the bilinear-warp explanation stands.

*** THE ISS-008 MASK IS GONE (2026-07-16). *** The dry tap was too hot by +20.5 dB (kDryGain, now
removed). The partial-blend HF EXCESS that inverted this issue's sign has largely collapsed:
    12.9k @BL=0.95: +24.4 -> +7.1 dB      12.9k @BL=0.90: +27.1 -> +8.2 dB
So BL=0.90/0.95 are now usable for measurement, though they still read a few dB of residual EXCESS
where BL=1.00 reads a DEFICIT — so treat the BL=1.00 trio as the primary evidence and use the partial
blends only as corroboration.

TWO CAPTURE CONSTRAINTS THAT STILL APPLY:
  - BLEND=0.50 has NO usable capture: the only file at that setting is corrupt (ISS-011). Its old
    "+54.4 dB @12.9k" reading is void — do not cite it.
  - This issue's own 8 kHz neighbourhood now has a live gate: V2IntegrationTest asserts hf8k < -24.0
    dB against §1's ~-40 dB target (it currently reads -26.6, a ~13 dB deficit — the same defect this
    issue tracks). Tighten that gate toward -40 dB as this lands; it is the regression net for this
    work.

Reproduce: python3.11 analysis/cascade_analysis.py (section C); python3.11 analysis/ab_report.py --filter V2 --os 8.
title: V2 12.5k/16k HF residual is cumulative bilinear warp
